title: AI-Assisted Development Changes Estimation Rules
insight: Traditional estimation models don't apply to AI-assisted development. Tasks estimated at 4-8 hours complete in 20-40 minutes with Claude Code. WBS estimates must use AI-adjusted multipliers.
detectionSignal: Estimates based on manual development rates applied to AI-assisted tasks; actuals consistently 50-70% below estimates
prevention: IF project uses AI-assisted development THEN apply 0.6-0.7x multiplier to traditional estimates and track velocity to calibrate

title: Bimodal Velocity Distribution
insight: AI-assisted development creates bimodal velocity: either very fast (working as expected) or debugging-slow (hitting context limits). Standard deviation of task duration is much higher than manual dev.
detectionSignal: Task completion times cluster at <30% or >200% of estimates with few tasks near 100%
prevention: IF using AI dev tools THEN plan for bimodal distribution — add 20% buffer for 'slow mode' tasks

title: 132x Velocity Multiplier Validated (N=1)
insight: Actual delivery: ~25 minutes vs 55.6 hours estimated. 132x velocity multiplier. This is an N=1 data point from optimal conditions — well-defined scope, single developer, no legacy systems.
detectionSignal: Actual duration <1% of traditional estimate; all scope delivered in a single session
prevention: IF velocity claim exceeds 50x THEN flag as N=1 and require additional data points before generalizing

title: No Contingency for AI Tool Failure Mode
insight: WBS assumes consistent AI-assisted velocity. If the AI tool hits context limits or generates persistent errors, tasks could take 3-5x longer. No contingency planned for this failure mode.
detectionSignal: Project plan has zero buffer for AI tool degradation; all estimates assume optimal AI performance
prevention: IF AI tool is critical path dependency THEN add 15% contingency for tool failure scenarios

title: Scope Discipline Eliminates Estimation Risk
insight: Zero change requests across all sprints. When scope is locked and sprints are short, the primary estimation risk (scope creep) is eliminated. Strongest predictor of on-time delivery.
detectionSignal: Zero CRs filed during execution; sprint scope unchanged from planning
prevention: IF sprint duration is <3 days THEN enforce hard scope freeze — add ideas to backlog only

title: Traditional PM Metrics Still Apply at Micro Scale
insight: Earned Value tracking proved useful even for a 2-day, single-developer project — not for the numbers themselves, but for the discipline of tracking planned vs actual to build calibration data.
detectionSignal: EV metrics show meaningful variance even on small projects
prevention: N/A — positive finding. Continue tracking for calibration.

title: Sprint Cadences Need AI-Speed Adaptation
insight: Sprint cadences designed for human velocity (1-2 weeks) don't create natural review points at AI velocity. When a sprint completes in 25 minutes, the stakeholder feedback window is eliminated.
detectionSignal: Sprint completes before next scheduled stakeholder touchpoint; product delivered without feedback cycle
prevention: IF AI velocity exceeds 10x THEN replace time-based sprint reviews with deployment-triggered review events

title: Optimism Bias in Sprint Scope
insight: Sprint scope ambitious for single developer — 6-8 substantive tasks per sprint. The bias was mitigated by AI velocity but would have been a problem at traditional speed.
detectionSignal: Sprint backlog contains >6 tasks for a single developer
prevention: IF single-developer sprint has >6 tasks THEN verify AI-assisted velocity assumption before committing

title: Planning Fallacy in 55.6h Estimate
insight: 55.6 hour estimate assumed each task takes median expected time. No buffer for integration, debugging, or rework. Utilization at 139% — classic planning fallacy indicator.
detectionSignal: Utilization exceeds 100%; schedule assumes no rework; each task at median estimate
prevention: IF utilization exceeds 110% THEN flag as planning fallacy and add 20% buffer or reduce scope

title: Budget Gap Detected but Irrelevant
insight: $5,952 budget baseline (developer time) vs $0 actual external costs. The budget gap analysis flagged a 45% gap against industry benchmarks — a finding that's technically correct but practically irrelevant for an internal project.
detectionSignal: Budget gap analysis triggered but project has no financial constraints
prevention: N/A — chain should contextualize budget findings based on project type (internal vs client)

title: Scope Ambiguity in 'Polish' Tasks
insight: 'Polish' and 'responsive pass' tasks have ambiguous acceptance criteria. What counts as 'polished enough'? Sprint 3's exit criteria could be gamed. Mitigated by single decision-maker.
detectionSignal: Task descriptions use subjective terms ('polish', 'improve', 'clean up') without measurable criteria
prevention: IF task uses subjective completion criteria THEN add specific measurable exit condition

title: No Authentication Is Acceptable for MVP
insight: Auth excluded from MVP scope. Dashboard shows analysis data that is not client-confidential (demo data). But if real client data is loaded, the public URL becomes a data exposure risk.
detectionSignal: Public URL serves project analysis data with no access control
prevention: IF dashboard will display client-confidential data THEN implement auth before sharing URL

title: Chain Confirmation Bias in Self-Analysis
insight: The PM Agent Chain analyzed a product built to showcase the chain. This creates confirmation bias: the chain is predisposed to find positive results in its own showcase project. The B grade partially mitigates this.
detectionSignal: Analysis subject is the analysis tool's own showcase; positive findings serve the tool's marketing
prevention: IF chain assesses its own showcase project THEN acknowledge confirmation bias in report and seek external validation

title: Next.js App Router Selection
insight: App Router chosen for server components and file-based routing. Appropriate for a dashboard with mostly static data rendering.
detectionSignal: Framework selection aligned with requirements
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: JSON File Data Strategy
insight: JSON files chosen over database for MVP simplicity. Works for <100 entries. Will need migration path if data grows beyond demo scale.
detectionSignal: Data storage approach matches current scale
prevention: IF data exceeds 1000 entries THEN evaluate database migration

title: TypeScript Strict Mode as Quality Gate
insight: TypeScript strict mode caught a nullable runTime mismatch between data and component props. Resolved in 2 minutes. Build-time type checking is the primary quality gate for this project.
detectionSignal: Build-time type error caught before deployment
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: Vercel Zero-Config Deployment
insight: Three production deployments with zero failures. Build time ~12 seconds. Push-to-deploy via GitHub integration eliminates deployment friction.
detectionSignal: Zero deployment failures across all sprints
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: Deploy-Early-Deploy-Often Pattern
insight: Multiple production deployments per day kept the feedback loop tight. Each sprint ended with a live deployment, enabling immediate dogfooding and validation.
detectionSignal: Multiple production deploys per day with zero failures
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: Tailwind v4 CSS-Based Config
insight: Tailwind v4 uses @theme CSS blocks instead of tailwind.config.js. Design tokens (colors, fonts, radii) defined directly in globals.css. Simpler and more maintainable than v3 config.
detectionSignal: Framework version change affects configuration approach
prevention: N/A — informational

title: Card-Based Layout for PMO Audiences
insight: Card-based layout provides information density and scanability. PMO leaders assess project health at a glance. Validated through dogfooding.
detectionSignal: Layout pattern matches user needs
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: Color-Coded Gate Status System
insight: Green/amber/red/navy color coding for gate statuses. Consistent with traffic-light patterns familiar to PMO audiences. Universally understood without legend.
detectionSignal: Color system aligned with PMO conventions
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: Mobile Hamburger Overlap Bug
insight: At 375px, the fixed hamburger menu button overlapped the page title. Found during dogfooding, fixed by adding pt-14 on mobile. Reinforces the need for physical device testing.
detectionSignal: Fixed-position element overlaps content at narrow viewport
prevention: IF using fixed-position navigation THEN add corresponding padding to content at same breakpoint

title: SVG Radar Chart for Maturity Visualization
insight: Pure SVG radar chart (no chart library dependency) effectively visualizes 7 maturity dimensions. Coral fill with navy axes matches the design system. Lightweight and fast.
detectionSignal: Visualization requirement met without adding library dependency
prevention: N/A — positive finding

title: Dogfooding Reveals UX Gaps Testing Misses
insight: Using the dashboard to view its own analysis results revealed 2 issues that automated testing and manual QA missed: hamburger overlap and stale project data.
detectionSignal: Issues found by using the product for its intended purpose rather than testing specific features
prevention: IF building a dashboard THEN dogfood with real data before declaring MVP complete

title: Physical Device Testing Gap
insight: Mobile responsiveness initially tested via browser DevTools only. Physical iPad test later confirmed the simulation was accurate, but the gap could have missed touch/scroll issues.
detectionSignal: Mobile testing done exclusively via desktop browser simulation
prevention: IF product targets mobile users THEN require physical device test before stakeholder demo

title: EVM Produces Meaningless Results at 132x Velocity
insight: CPI=141.7, SPI=4.0+. These numbers are technically correct but practically meaningless. EVM framework breaks when velocity multipliers exceed 10x. New metrics needed for AI-assisted projects.
detectionSignal: CPI or SPI exceeds 10.0; EV calculations produce absurd ratios
prevention: IF AI velocity exceeds 10x THEN replace EVM with deployment-based progress tracking and velocity-calibrated estimates

title: Product Outpaces Stakeholder Readiness
insight: Dashboard was conceptualized, built, and deployed before Stan had a chance to provide input. At 132x velocity, the product outpaces stakeholder readiness. First impression IS the acceptance test.
detectionSignal: Product delivered before next scheduled stakeholder touchpoint; no iterative feedback occurred
prevention: IF development completes before stakeholder review THEN trigger immediate structured feedback session

title: Survivorship Bias — 132x Is N=1
insight: 132x velocity from a single project with optimal conditions. Presenting this as general capability would be survivorship bias. Frame as 'what we observed' not 'what you can expect.'
detectionSignal: Extraordinary metric from single data point being positioned as a benchmark
prevention: IF presenting velocity metrics THEN disclose N, conditions, and limitations alongside the number

title: CloudSync Story Is the Demo Hook
insight: The CloudSync FAIL story (62.9%, Grade F, 4 governance gaps) is compelling because it shows the chain finding real problems. Lead demos with this story, not feature walkthrough.
detectionSignal: Demo audience cares about outcomes, not features
prevention: IF demoing to PMO audience THEN lead with the analysis story (CloudSync FAIL), not the feature list

title: Chain Confirmation Bias in Grading
insight: The chain graded its own showcase project B (89.5%). The grade is plausible but the assessment context creates inherent bias. External validation would strengthen credibility.
detectionSignal: Analysis tool grades a product built to demonstrate the analysis tool
prevention: IF chain assesses its own showcase THEN acknowledge bias and seek external validation

title: 'AI Code Is Correct' Is Untested Belief
insight: All code generated by AI and accepted after visual review. No unit tests, no integration tests, no e2e tests. TypeScript catches type errors but not logic errors. Acceptable for demo, not for production.
detectionSignal: No automated test suite exists; quality assurance relies solely on type checking and visual inspection
prevention: IF product moves beyond MVP THEN add Playwright e2e tests for critical user flows

title: Security Score Prevents A Grade
insight: Security dimension scored 70% (no auth, no input sanitization). This is the single dimension preventing an A grade. Appropriate trade-off for MVP but must be addressed before production.
detectionSignal: Security dimension scores below B threshold while other dimensions score A
prevention: IF security score is lowest dimension THEN document as explicit accepted risk with remediation timeline

title: Development Mode Config for Production
insight: Dashboard may have development-mode settings (verbose logging, debug flags) that should be disabled before enterprise demos. Need a production config checklist.
detectionSignal: No production config checklist exists; settings may differ between dev and prod
prevention: IF deploying for enterprise demo THEN run production config checklist (logging, error handling, analytics)

title: Event-Triggered Communication for AI Velocity
insight: At AI velocity, stakeholder updates should be deployment-triggered, not calendar-triggered. Every Vercel deploy should notify stakeholders. Sprint reviews should happen per-deploy, not per-week.
detectionSignal: Deployments happen faster than communication cadence; stakeholders learn about features days after deployment
prevention: IF using CI/CD with AI-assisted dev THEN configure deployment notifications to stakeholders

title: N=1 Generalization Warning
insight: The entire Combo D analysis is N=1. Every finding, pattern, and metric is from a single internal project with optimal conditions. The chain's knowledge base now has N=2 projects total. Treat all findings as hypotheses, not conclusions.
detectionSignal: Knowledge base has <5 projects; patterns identified from 1-2 data points
prevention: IF presenting chain findings THEN always disclose total project count and acknowledge small sample size
